title: achillea maritima subsp. atlantica
intro: Achillea maritima subsp. atlantica, also known as Atlantic Yarrow, is a subspecies of the Yarrow plant, a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate conditions such as digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that provide various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing menstrual pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Atlantic Yarrow can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or by combining them with other herbs to create teas, salves, and ointments that can be used to promote overall well-being.
